---
title: "Human vs Machine"
subtitle: "Validity vs Hallucination"
date: 20 May 2025
format: 
  beamer:
    incremental: false
---

## **Pre-workshop activities**

-   Search for studies that would answer the following question:
    -   For women with recurrent vulvovaginal candidiasis, does oral compared with topical maintenance antifungal therapy, improve quality of life?
-   Assess the quality of a relevant reference.
-   Create a case report form for your new study.

## The Bottom line

![](images/idiot.jpeg){fig-align="center"}

# Finding relevant references

## Bibliographic databases

| Database | Provider       | Platform         |
|:---------|:---------------|:-----------------|
| MEDLINE  | NLM            | PubMed           |
| MEDLINE  | Clarivate      | Web of Science   |
| CENTRAL  | Wiley          | Cochrane Library |
| Embase   | Wolters Kluwer | Ovid             |
| Embase   | Elsevier       | Embase           |

: {tbl-colwidths="\[25,25,50\]"}

## DB, explained

-   consists of a 2D table

    -   rows: records.

    -   columns: fields (each field = a specific type of data)

-   Fields may contain either free or controlled data

| Field            | tag    |
|:-----------------|:-------|
| Title            | \[ti\] |
| Abstract         | \[ab\] |
| MeSH terms       | \[mh\] |
| Publication type | \[pt\] |

: {tbl-colwidths="\[50,50\]"}

## Concepts and terms

-   Concepts

    -   Population

    -   Intervention

    -   Study design

-   Terms are synonyms for each concept: browse the MeSH database

# Quality assessment

## Combining trash

![](images/trash_metaanalysis.png)

## Domains

![](images/rob.png)

# Essential documents

## The CRF

::: nonincremental
-   CRF is Mandatory to

    -   collect data that can be verified and used for analysis

    -   guarantee the quality of analyses

    -   validate and replicate findings

-   CRF must

    -   reflect the protocol (i.e. collect the variables of the study)

    -   be developed and tested in advance
:::

## Designing a CRF

::: nonincremental
-   Use of clear, simple, neutral and concise questions

-   Provide clear instructions in bold and italics

-   Use visual cues e.g. check boxes to clearly indicate place & format of data

-   Collect raw data e.g. weight and height rather than BMI

-   Specify the unit of measurement

-   Indicate the number of decimal places

-   CRFs must include a front page with consent check box and eligibility check
:::

## Completing a CRF

::: nonincremental
-   Always use a black or blue ballpoint pen

-   Never leave blank spaces

-   Avoid using the ambiguous phrase ‘NA’.

-   Never over-write an entry.

    -   In case of error Cross out the wrong text.

    -   The wrong text Must remain visible.

    -   Write the correct answer besides and Sign.

-   Never write the patient’s full name
:::

## Poorly designed CRF

Date of visit: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Blood pressure: \_\_\_\_\_ / \_\_\_\_\_

Pulse: \_\_\_\_\_

Temperature: \_\_\_\_\_

Respiration: \_\_\_\_\_

# Post-workshop activities

## Well designed CRF

Data of Birth (DD/MM/YYYY) $□$$□$/ $□$$□$/ $□$$□$$□$$□$

Gender M $□$ 1 – F $□$ 2

Height (cm) $□$$□$$□$.$□$

Weight (Kg) $□$$□$$□$.$□$$□$

Smoker. Yes $□$ 1 – No $□$ 2

## Hand-made

-   The comprehensive search strategy

-   The quality assessment

# Thank you - AN
